title: Psychotria rufocalyx
intro: Psychotria rufocalyx is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the "red-haired Psychotria" due to its reddish-brown hairs. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatic pain, and respiratory issues, highlighting its potential as an anti-inflammatory and antipyretic agent. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria rufocalyx include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to alleviate various health conditions.
taxonomy: Psychotria rufocalyx is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This plant belongs to the genus Psychotria, which comprises over 1,800 species of flowering plants, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, consisting of over 600 genera and 13,000 species. Psychotria rufocalyx is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it inhabits a variety of ecosystems, including rainforests and cloud forests. The classification of Psychotria rufocalyx highlights its unique position within the Rubiaceae family and its importance in the tropical ecosystems of the Americas.
